title: rosa rubiginosa var. trachyphylla
intro: Rosa rubiginosa var. trachyphylla, also known as the fragrant or scotch rose, is a shrub-like plant belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from its leaves, stems, and flowers. The health benefits of Rosa rubiginosa var. trachyphylla include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for skin issues and digestive problems. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional.
